differential_diagnoses: Disseminated Bartonella henselae infection (Cat Scratch Disease)
Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD) with occult systemic infection
Chronic Nonbacterial Osteomyelitis (CNO) / Chronic Recurrent Multifocal Osteomyelitis (CRMO)
next_steps: Dihydrorhodamine (DHR) flow cytometry test to screen for Chronic Granulomatous Disease
Bartonella henselae and quintana serology (IgG/IgM) and PCR
Biopsy of bone or liver lesion for histology, microbiology (Fungal/AFB/Aerobic/Anaerobic cultures), and molecular testing
Ophthalmologic examination for neuroretinitis or other stigmata of cat-scratch disease
Detailed CBC with peripheral blood smear and LDH levels to further assess for hematologic malignancy
QuantiFERON-TB Gold or Tuberculin skin test
